# Rule: Consumables, Loot, and Economy

## Scope

Use this rule for consumable handling, reward tables, coin/ether flow, and economic pacing.

## Consolidated Rule

Consumables are limited by ready-access slots and action timing, while loot scales by encounter difficulty and level pressure. Economy is a balance lever for survivability, gear progression, and resource attrition.

## Consumables Handling

- Ready access: up to 4 consumable types, up to 4 quantity each.
- Most use minor actions; some use reactions if text permits.
- One consumable use per action.
- Refill from inventory out of combat with time.

## Reward Pacing

- Easy/standard/difficult encounter bands map to expected coin/ether/scroll outcomes.
- Treasure horde outcomes are campaign spikes and should be intentional.
- Equipment tier access should roughly follow recommended level pacing.

## Economic Pressure Tools

- Draining resources before major encounters is expected design.
- Rest/travel constraints and profession income influence campaign economy rhythm.

## Adjudication Notes

- Track both immediate loot and downstream power impact.
- If party power is spiking, shift rewards toward situational consumables and narrative assets over raw permanent upgrades.
